patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD) validated 6+ consecutive times with zero exceptions: PHI@PIT (-5.52pp), TOR@DET (-1.72pp), NYY@NYM (-7.46pp), SD@SEA (-3.23pp) all correctly zero-stake today — even large F-signals (F1*=2.38 for NYY, F3=4874 for TOR) don't override when edge is negative.

PLATINUM tier is 0-for-4 in directional accuracy across full postgame archive (SF@LAD x2, KC@CHW, SEA@HOU all wrong); both today's PLATINUM picks (BOS@ATL, SF@OAK) carry SP-unconfirmed or bullpen-fatigue concerns warranting one-tier reduction — neither meets HARD CAP 3 thresholds but PLATINUM scrutiny mandates extra review.

Three-signal GOLD bar (F-signals + positive edge + f5_full_delta < 0.15 + no stacked hard-cap flags) is 4-for-4 in archive; CIN@CLE today is the only game meeting all criteria — confidence_downgrade+bullpen_fatigue flags present but no secondary downgrade signal (delta=0.04, fair_prob=0.57), confirming 05-12 rule that flags alone don't mandate DOWNGRADE on positive-edge picks.

Coors Field produces legitimate extreme Stage 1/2 gaps driven by park effects, not model artifacts: ARI@COL f5_full_delta=0.3958 (Freeland dominant in F5, Coors bullpen vulnerable in innings 6-9) with market giving ARI 53.58% — market agrees with ARI direction, distinguishing park-effect from calibration artifact requiring OVERRIDE.

F3_swing_take_gap values 3x+ above typical slate maxima (~1800) on both PLATINUM picks today (BOS@ATL: 5596, SF@OAK: 6350) — while not definitively artifacts (ATL's lineup vs weak BOS SP is plausible), combined with SP-unconfirmed and PLATINUM history, these reinforce the DOWNGRADE-to-GOLD decision.

PHI @ PIT.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 correctly fires: edge=-5.52pp collapses GOLD to B- (score 4→1), zeroing stake. Pre-cap A- driven by F1_xera_gap=2.20* (Ashcraft thin Statcast sample) and strong PQI=+22.5 confirming PIT. Aaron Nola (PHI) is a top-tier ace — the market has correctly priced his quality edge, producing the negative edge. F1 asterisk marks the SP comparison as unreliable. HARD CAP 1 is validated 6+ times; PIT direction credible but not stakeable against Nola.

BAL @ WSH.reasoning: Grade D (score=-1): SP edge 3.00 xERA AGAINST WSH (Shane Baz rated substantially better than Zack Littell) and PQI AGAINST pick (Δ=-26.4). Only F3=2637.6 supports WSH direction. Market gives WSH only 42.77% (fair_prob=0.4277) — market shares SP skepticism about WSH. Both quantitative quality signals (SP, PQI) disagree with the full-prob pick. D grade and no stake correctly applied.

TOR @ DET.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 fires: edge=-1.72pp collapses GOLD to B- (score 3→1), zeroing stake. TOR wins F5 at 69.15% (Yesavage dominates early vs Hanifee) but f5_full_delta=0.1692 means the late-game thesis relies on TOR's bullpen — and bullpen_fatigue=True with confidence_downgrade=True stacks the concern. Per 05-09 postgame, Stage 1/2 gap 16.3pp + bullpen_fatigue is a reliable contra-indicator for the pick direction. Negative edge confirms market holds better information. No stake.

CIN @ CLE.reasoning: CONFIRM GOLD — three-signal bar cleanly met (4-for-4 in archive). Tanner Bibee (CLE) vs Andrew Abbott (CIN): SP edge +0.93 xERA confirms CLE. F1_xera_gap=1.30* backed by F5_bullpen_gap=+0.84 satisfies HARD CAP 5 (secondary signal prevents sole-F1* demotion). Edge=+7.0pp in [4,15]pp band. f5_full_delta=0.0409 well below 15pp threshold. PQI confirms (+14.9). confidence_downgrade+bullpen_fatigue present but no secondary downgrade signal (delta=0.04, fair_prob=0.569) — per 05-12 rule, stacked flags alone don't mandate DOWNGRADE on positive-edge GOLD.

MIA @ TB.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: edge=+0.67pp outside [4,15]pp band. Grade D (score=-2): SP edge 0.39 xERA AGAINST TB (Junk rated above Scholtens), PQI AGAINST pick (Δ=-9.4). confidence_downgrade+bullpen_fatigue both stacked. TB is 28-14 (strong team) but today's matchup signals all run against the direction. Grade D with no F-signals firing and both quality signals negative. CONFIRM SKIP.

BOS @ ATL.reasoning: DOWNGRADE PLATINUM→GOLD. Spencer Strider confirmed starting (live API) but has 0 Statcast pitches — SP edge unconfirmed, F1 signal cannot fire, producing f5_full_delta=0.1831 above 15pp concern threshold (ATL wins F5 only 48.23%). ATL's full-game thesis requires bullpen carry — no bullpen_fatigue flag mitigates this. ATL 30-14 vs BOS 18-25, F2/F3=5596.0, and PQI (+10.0) support ATL direction strongly at GOLD. PLATINUM is unjustified without verified Strider Statcast data; PLATINUM is 0-for-4 in archive direction accuracy.

NYY @ NYM.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 fires: edge=-7.46pp collapses GOLD to B- (score 4→1), zeroing stake. Pre-cap A- with F1_xera_gap=2.38* (Schlittler thin sample) + F2_xwoba_gap=0.027. f5_full_delta=0.1376 with confidence_downgrade=True — HARD CAP 4 threshold 0.12 met but cap needs BOTH conditions (delta here is 0.1376 vs confidence_downgrade=True would trigger if combined correctly, but HARD CAP 1 already fired first). NYY is 27-17, real quality advantage, but Clay Holmes (NYM converted reliever-starter) creates genuine matchup uncertainty market has priced. No stake.

CHC @ CHW.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied despite A- grade (score=4). fair_prob=0.4359 is BELOW the 0.42 floor — market prices CHW at only 43.59% vs model's 55.36% (+11.77pp edge). Per 05-12 rule, stacked flags (confidence_downgrade+bullpen_fatigue) require secondary signal to mandate DOWNGRADE; fair_prob < 0.42 IS that secondary signal. Market strongly prefers CHC (28-16) over CHW (22-21). Edward Cabrera vs Sean Burke: market disagrees with the model's SP edge read. CONFIRM SKIP; SKIP tier pipeline decision is well-supported.

TEX @ HOU.reasoning: Grade D (score=-1): SP edge 0.79 xERA AGAINST HOU (Jack Leiter rated better than Spencer Arrighetti), PQI AGAINST pick (Δ=-10.1). acute_roster=True flags a live lineup concern for HOU. confidence_downgrade=True. Edge=+6.03pp but grade D means staking is inappropriate. HOU is 17-28 (worst record on slate). Leiter has been a quality TEX SP per prior postgame. All structural signals favor TEX; HOU direction at 55.57% with SP and PQI running counter is a low-conviction D-grade SKIP.

KC @ STL.reasoning: Grade D (score=-3), lowest on slate. SP edge 0.99 xERA AGAINST STL (Michael Wacha rated substantially above Dustin May) and PQI AGAINST pick (Δ=-17.9). f5_full_delta=0.2403 is large — KC wins F5 at 59.65% (Wacha dominant), STL needs full-game reversal. Market is nearly neutral (fair_prob=0.5093) despite model's STL at 64.38%. OVERRIDE to KC considered but no stacked pipeline flags (confidence_downgrade=False, no acute_roster) — four-condition OVERRIDE trigger incomplete. CONFIRM SKIP/D.

ARI @ COL.reasoning: CONFIRM SKIP/D — Coors Field park effect, not model artifact. Kyle Freeland at Coors vs Merrill Kelly: COL wins F5 at 89.21% (Freeland historically dominant at altitude), producing f5_full_delta=0.3958. But market gives ARI 53.58% (fair_prob=0.5358) — market agrees with ARI full-game direction because Coors bullpen collapses in innings 6-9 are systematic. Both model and market point to ARI; this is NOT a calibration artifact requiring OVERRIDE to COL. D grade (score=-4) correctly reflects structural uncertainty. CONFIRM no-stake.

LAD @ LAA.reasoning: Blake Snell confirmed as LAD starter (live MLB API: Jack Kochanowicz for LAA). However Snell has 0 Statcast pitches season-to-date — 100+ pitch threshold for SP quality scoring unmet. PENDING designation correct; F1 SP edge cannot be computed without Statcast data. LAD is 26-18 vs LAA 16-28, directional lean to LAD qualitatively, but no formalizable pick without Snell quality score. PENDING protocol validated across 5+ prior archive instances.

SF @ OAK.reasoning: DOWNGRADE PLATINUM→GOLD. Score=7 (slate's highest) with F2_xwoba_gap=0.033 + F3=6350.2, edge=+8.44pp, PQI=+6.2, f5_full_delta=0.0985 (OAK improves in full game vs F5). F3=6350.2 is 3.5× prior slate maxima (~1800) — elevated size adds uncertainty. Per 05-12: stacked flags alone don't mandate GOLD DOWNGRADE, but PLATINUM warrants extra scrutiny; one-tier reduction appropriate. OAK direction well-supported at GOLD.
